basics.name: Aarjan Budathoki
basics.label: Electronics, Communication and Information Engineering Student
basics.summary: Bachelor in Electronics, Communication and Information Engineering at Tribhuvan University, Institute of Engineering, Pulchowk Campus

work.name: LogicTronix [FPGA Design & Machine Learning Company]
work.summary: Developed robust pipelines for data acquisition & visualization of Velodyne 3D LiDAR sensor data using the Point Cloud Library (PCL).
highlights: Developed robust pipelines for data acquisition & visualization of Velodyne 3D LiDAR sensor data using the Point Cloud Library (PCL)
Annotated custom 3D point cloud datasets using 3D bounding box labeling tools(Latte)
Implemented voxel grid down-sampling algorithms to optimized point cloud density for computationally efficient processing
Implemented RANSAC-based ground plane segmentation in C++ to effectively isolate dynamic objects such as pedestrians and vehicles from static ground surfaces
Implemented DBSCAN clustering algorithm in C++ for object detection and classification, targeting pedestrians, vehicles and traffic objects
Developed and optimized Kd-Tree data structure from scratch in C++ to accelerate nearest-neighbor search operations on large-scale point clouds

work.name: Abhiyan Engineering Nepal Pvt. Ltd.
work.summary: Contributed to hardware system development for the Automatic Sand Level Measurement Embedded System (PSLM Device)
highlights: Contributed to hardware system development for the Automatic Sand Level Measurement Embedded System (PSLM Device), focusing on sensor integration, circuit design, and system prototyping

work.name: Robotics Club, IOE Pulchowk Campus
work.summary: Implemented kinematics modeling and odometry of four omniwheeled motion robot
highlights: Implemented kinematics modeling and odometry of four omniwheeled motion robot
Implemented PID controllers for position and velocity tuning of brushed and brushless DC motors
Design & Fabrication of main controller board of the R2 BOT and power supply of bots in ABU ROBOCON 2022

Acquired proficiency in OpenCV, applying fundamental image processing techniques (thresholding, geometric transformations, filtering) and advanced computer vision (object detection, feature matching, video analysis)

highlights: Bachelor in Electronics, Communication and Information Engineering

projects.name: Greenhouse Management using AWS IoT and Yocto Embedded Linux
projects.summary: Major Project - Developed an Embedded Linux OS for Raspberry Pi 4 (64-bit) using Yocto Project
highlights: Embedded Linux: Developed an Embedded Linux OS for Raspberry Pi 4 (64-bit) using Yocto Project, featuring tools like git, python3, opkg, connman, mosquitto, and systemctl. Enabled UART, modifiable root filesystem, and WIC image support for deployment

projects.name: Self-Driving Car
projects.summary: Minor Project - Integrated ros2 control for precise control of car with ackermann wheel steering
highlights: Integrated ros2 control for precise control of car with ackermann wheel steering
Implemented SLAM algorithms to generate accurate Lidar-based maps of static environments
Incorporated nav2 stack for autonomous navigation and real-time path planning
Real-time object detection using a YOLOv8 model on live camera
Transformed and projected 2d lidar scan onto the camera image and its visualization

projects.name: Elephant and Rabbit Robot
projects.summary: ABU Robocon 2023 (Cambodia) - Used the scikit-learn framework DBSCAN clustering algorithm for detecting field poles
highlights: Used the scikit-learn framework DBSCAN clustering algorithm and Nearest Neighbors library for detecting field poles using 2D LiDAR point cloud data to accurately identify and localize the nearest pole within the game environment

projects.name: R1 and R2 Robot
projects.summary: ABU Robocon 2022 (India) - Design & Fabrication of main controller board
highlights: Design & Fabrication of main controller board of the R2 BOT and power supply of bots in ABU ROBOCON 2022

projects.name: Maze Generation and Solving Algorithm
projects.summary: Data Structures and Algorithms Project - Implemented DFS and shortest path algorithms
highlights: Implemented Depth-First Search (DFS) algorithm for procedural maze generation
Applied shortest pathfinding algorithms, including Dijkstra's Algorithm and A* Search, for efficient maze solving

projects.name: Self-Balancing Robot
projects.summary: Instrumentation Project - Implemented PID Control for Angle Equilibrium using IMU
highlights: Implemented PID Control for Angle Equilibrium using IMU
